changelog:
Skill project-additions marker: skill .md files can now carry a <!-- @@project-additions --> line; everything below it is adopter-owned and preserved under template-updater --merge (the template head above still refreshes), the same way agent Soul Zones survive sync. Closes the hole where any project-specific guidance added to a template skill was clobbered on the next sync — generalizing the one-off skills/brand-guidelines/** project-exception into a section-level mechanism for any skill. No marker (or no --merge) = plain overwrite, exactly as before. Commands that customize template skills (/review:specialize, /sweep, /review:evolve-skills) now write project additions below the marker.

--- v4.84 ---
settings.json merge: statusLine is now template-owned (it's a pointer at the template-owned core/statusline.sh, like hooks — so the command line tracks the template instead of freezing at the adopter's scaffolded value). A project that genuinely wants a custom status line overrides it in settings.local.json, which the sync always skips. Template-owned settings keys are now hooks + plansDirectory + statusLine; project-owned are permissions + env + $schema + any unknown keys.

--- v4.83 ---
status.js --regen-master now keeps the master tracker internally consistent: it recomputes the Stories denominator from the same per-epic checklists it reads Done from (per-epic, per-phase, and grand Total), so a /todo-split epic (more scaffolded stories than the backlog planned) no longer shows Done exceeding the phase total (the 'Done 33 of 16' drift). Unscaffolded epics keep their planned count as the denominator fallback. Table columns are now resolved by HEADER NAME rather than hardcoded indices, so a master tracker that drifts from the canonical column layout (e.g. an extra 'Ph' column in the Epic Index) refreshes the correct Status column instead of clobbering Est. template-updater gains a settings.json escape hatch: under --merge the template now OWNS the hooks block + default plansDirectory (so newly-shipped hooks finally wire themselves into adopters) while the project KEEPS permissions, env, statusLine, $schema, and any unknown keys; settings.local.json + update-config.json stay always-skipped.
